chapters.summary: Council staff calls the meeting to order, welcomes attendees to the Finance Committee vote, and reminds everyone to silence electronics and not approach the dais.
chapters.title: Call to order and housekeeping

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Now guys, settle down.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you for your patience.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Good afternoon, good afternoon.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Welcome to the New York City Council vote on the Committee on Finance.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: At this time, please silence all electronics and do not approach the dais.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Chair, you may begin.

chapters.summary: Chair Linda Lee opens the meeting, announces the vote to adopt the FY2027 budget, and acknowledges all committee members present including Brooks-Powers, Feliz, Avilés, Restler, Hudson, Mealy, Narcisse, Williams, Aldebol, Maloney, Sanchez, Wong, and Morano.
chapters.title: Chair Lee welcomes members and acknowledges committee

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Good afternoon and welcome to, ooh, feedback.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Welcome to today's Finance Committee meeting where we will be voting to adopt the fiscal 2027 budget.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Councilmember Linda Lee, Chair of the Finance Committee, and I would like to acknowledge my fellow committee members present.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Councilmember Brooks Powers, Councilmember Feliz, Councilmember Aviles, Council Majority Leader Breu, Councilmember Hudson, Councilmember, wait, no, he's not here, sorry.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Councilmember Mealy, Councilmember Narcisse, Councilmember Ressler, Deputy Speaker Williams, Councilmember Aldabal, Councilmember Maloney, Councilmember Jay Sanchez, Councilmember Wong, and Councilmember Marano.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Yay.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Sorry, it's going to be a little long.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: I think this is the longest one I've ever had, so I will try to be quick.

chapters.summary: Chair Lee thanks Speaker Julie Menin for her leadership, and expresses deep appreciation for the Finance Division staff including Director Nathan Toth, Managing Director Jonathan Rosenberg, deputy directors, chief economists, chief counsel, and senior counsel who prepared briefings and reports for the budget.
chapters.title: Chair Lee thanks Speaker and Finance Division leadership team

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Today, the Finance Committee will take the necessary actions for the City Council to adopt a $125.8 billion budget for fiscal year 2027.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: To begin, I would like to thank Speaker Julie-Menin for her leadership throughout this entire budget process.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: The Speaker...

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: and the council remained committed to reaching a budget that keeps vulnerable families housed, invests in New Yorkers, and safeguards our city's long-term fiscal future.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: I also want to thank the Council Finance Division for their hard work in their preparations of the briefings, presentations, and reports which went into crafting this budget, and we deeply,

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: deeply appreciate the dedication of all the financial analysts and economists who got us this vote today.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: A very special thank you to the Finance Division's leadership team who spearheaded the effort.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Finance Director Nathan Toth.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Managing Director Jonathan Rosenberg, Deputy Directors Amra Adev, Chima Obesher, Paul Simone, and Isha Wright, Chief Economist and Assistant Director Dilara Dimnaku,

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Supervising Economist Paul Sturm and Andrew Wilber, Assistant Directors Alia Ali, Julia Haramis, Florentine Kabor, Daniel Krup, Caitlin O'Hagan, James Reyes, and Jack Story,

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Chief Counsel Nicholas Connell, Senior Counsel Nadia Jean-Francois, my committee counsel Brian Sarfo, I don't know why I feel like I have to do that, my senior advisor, sorry,

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Athena Say, and the many finance analysts, economists, and support staff who have worked behind the scenes to bring everything together.

chapters.summary: Chair Lee gives special recognition to the finance administrative staff and legislative documents unit, naming Nicole Anderson, Michelle Bhatti, and others, as well as the sergeants at arms and her own district office staff for keeping operations running during budget season.
chapters.title: Chair Lee thanks administrative staff and district office team

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: I want to make special notice of the finance administrative staff and the legislative documents unit that pull everything together to ensure that this budget process was seamless for all of us.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you to Nicole Anderson, Michelle Bhatti,

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Alexandra Pascal, Jonathan Ettricks, Ruthie Delfranco, Jillian Jeffries, Yvette Molina, Billy Martin, and Matthew DiStefano.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: The amazing sergeants at arms, yay!

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Sorry.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: They keep things orderly and safe on either side of Broadway throughout our preliminary and executive hearings, parade season, and everything in between.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you, Carl, Raphael, and the entire team.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And last but not least, a special shout out to my district office chief of staff, Keith Felsenfeld, deputy chief of staff, Daniel Sparrow,

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: legislative and budget director, John Wani, and my district manager, Sila Asa, for holding down the fort for District 23 and for being instrumental during this budget season.

chapters.summary: Chair Lee outlines the budget's key investments including $14.4 million for parks, $1 million for libraries, $4.5 million for ACT teams, $11 million for IMT, $2.5 million for crisis respite centers, $12 million for home-delivered meals, and $86.4 million restored for immigrant legal services, emphasizing a balanced budget without service cuts or drastic tax increases.
chapters.title: Chair Lee highlights key FY27 budget investments

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: This budget represents the Council's commitment to achieve a balanced budget without cutting services, drawing down on reserves, or imposing drastic property tax increases.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Further, this budget includes key investments in programs such as City FEPS, Fair Fairs, New York City Kids Rise.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: All these programs will aid families in preparing for the future and tackle the issue of affordability.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: We successfully protected and achieved funding of $14.4 million for our parks, maintaining and optimizing our green spaces, an additional $1 million for our libraries

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: continuing to sustain our seven-day service, and a continued commitment for the mental health and well-being of New Yorkers with $4.5 million for the city's ACT teams, $11 million for IMT, and step-down mobile treatment centers.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: The council was also able to restore $2.5 million in funding for crisis respite centers, proven evidence-based programs staffed by clinicians and peer counselors.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Lastly, this budget also secured $12 million to increase the reimbursements of nonprofits providing culturally competent home-delivered meals

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: seven days a week and restored $86.4 million for nonprofits delivering immigrant legal services, ensuring that New Yorkers have trusted legal support when needed most.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: We are delivering a balanced on-time budget, restoring our reserves, and still investing in making our city safer, healthier, and more affordable without adding additional burdens for New Yorkers.

chapters.summary: Chair Lee lists the nine categories of items requiring a vote: FY27 expense budget, contract budget, capital budget (Reso A and B), expense and revenue budget modifications, community development program, three property tax resolutions, four late payment interest rate resolutions, and a transparency resolution, plus reminders about Schedule C disclosures.
chapters.title: Chair Lee outlines agenda items for committee vote

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Now let's turn to adopting this budget.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Before we get to the vote, I'd like to discuss the specific items that we will vote on today.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: All members received a budget packet by the Committee, Council, and Finance team that contains all the budget-related legislation that must be voted on by the committee and, again, by the full council at the stated meeting.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: So I'm going to just list the items that are on our agenda today that require vote.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Number one, resolution approving the FY27 expense budget.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Number two, resolution approving the FY27 contract budget.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Number three, resolutions approving the FY27 capital budget, Reso A and Reso B.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Number four, resolution approving an expense budget modification, which closes out FY26.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Number five, resolution approving.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: approving a revenue budget modification, which closes out FY26.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Number six, resolution approving the proposed FY27 community development program, reallocation of 52nd year funds, and proposed 53rd year program.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Number seven, three property tax resolutions.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Current base proportion, adjusted base proportion, and the resolution fixing the tax rates are all interlocking resolutions that together are necessary for adopting the FY27 property tax rates.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Number eight, four resolutions in relation to real property tax late payment interest rates for FY27.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Number nine, a transparency resolution.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: The other documents in the packet which do not require a separate vote are Schedule C and a list of the terms and conditions included in the expense budget for FY27.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: As a reminder to members, Schedule C is a schedule of the expense and contract budgets, and the appropriations for the organizations listed in Schedule C are funded by the expense and contract budgets.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Council members will have to sign a disclosure form indicating whether or not a conflict exists with any of the groups listed in Schedule C or Reso A, which details the designations of capital discretionary funds.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: A separate disclosure form must be completed for the transparency resolution.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: If any council member has a potential conflict of interest with any of the organizations included, he or she has the opportunity to disclose the conflict at the time of their

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: If you have not yet signed those disclosure forms, staff from the General Counsel's office are available to guide you through the process.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: So please contact one of them before you vote.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: As a further reminder, please disclose any conflicts you may have with proposed subcontractors that are used by any of the organizations sponsored.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: These disclosures must be made before the subcontractor can be approved.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: So those are all of our items for today.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: I will now have Billy Martin, the committee clerk, call the roll.

chapters.summary: Committee clerk Billy Martin calls the roll with all items coupled. Chair Lee votes aye on all. Council Member Restler discloses that his wife works at the Mayor's Office of Management and Budget and his mother serves on boards of funded organizations, voting aye. Chair Lee then discloses that her children are associated with DOE and her husband with Korean Community Services.
chapters.title: Roll call begins: Chair Lee and Restler vote with disclosures

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Oh, where's Billy?

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Oh, okay.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: All right.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Yay.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay, so we will now have Billy Martin, the committee clerk, call the roll.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Hello, everybody.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: William Martin, committee clerk, roll call vote, committee on finance.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: All items are coupled.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Chair Lee.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Ressler.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: I am disclosing for the record that my wife works at the mayor's office of management of the budget, and I'm very proud of her, and my mother serves on the board of the Mercantile Library Association of the City of New York and the Brooklyn Heights Association.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: They are funded in this budget that we are adopting today, and I vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Chair Lee.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Sorry, I didn't realize I had to disclose now.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay, sorry.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the New York City Department of Education is funded in the budget we are adopting, and my children are associated with that entity, and that Korean Community Services is funded in the budget we are adopting, and my husband is associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Gennaro.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Brooks Powers.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Please come back to me.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Oh, sure.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Sorry.

chapters.summary: Council Member Feliz discloses associations with CUNY and Volunteers of America and votes aye. Abreu votes aye. Avilés discloses associations with Uprose, DOE, and Parks and Recreation, voting aye. Hudson discloses associations with Brooklyn Museum, Botanical Garden, Academy of Music, and other cultural institutions, voting aye.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record that the City University of New York and Volunteers of America are funded in the budget we're adopting, and I and also my sibling and parent, respectively, are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: With that said, I would, I and all, want to congratulate Speaker Menon, Finance Chair Lee, and all my colleagues for passing a budget that works for New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Abraiu.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Ivorai.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Avilets.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the Brooklyn Museum, Botanical Garden, Academy of Music, Museum of the City of New York, New York City Commission on Gender Equity, and Unlocal are funded in the budget we are adopting, and I, my spouse, and my business associate, respectively, are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Marte.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee

chapters.summary: Council Member Narcisse discloses associations with NYU, Brookdale Hospital, and Hope Incorporated through her partner and child, voting aye and thanking Speaker Menin and Chair Lee. Council Member Williams discloses associations with Children's Rescue Fund, CUNY, and Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, voting aye.
chapters.title: Council Members Narcisse and Williams vote with disclosures

utterances.speaker: Council Staff

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse

utterances.speaker: Council Staff

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee

Maloney discloses her sister serves on the board of the New York Foundation for Senior Citizens and her parent is associated with CUNY, the Floating Hospital, 92nd Street Y, and Metropolitan Opera, voting aye. Sanchez votes aye with no disclosures.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee

chapters.summary: Council Member Wong asks for clarification on bill numbers for the late payment interest rate resolutions, citing 2274, 2176, and 2182. After discussion with staff, he votes yes on all items except the 6% interest rate resolution, on which he votes no.
chapters.title: Council Member Wong questions bill numbers, votes no on one interest rate item

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Wong.

utterances.speaker: Phil Wong
utterances.text: Before voting, can I have the bill numbers for the late payment interest for the resolution for assessed values of $250K or less, and $250K to $450K, and those the value over $450K?

utterances.speaker: Phil Wong
utterances.text: I have them as 2274, 2176, and 2182.

utterances.speaker: Phil Wong
utterances.text: Is that right, or the numbers change?

utterances.speaker: Phil Wong
utterances.text: Do you know?

utterances.speaker: Phil Wong
utterances.text: Okay, so, okay, my vote is yes on all except one.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff

utterances.speaker: Council Staff

utterances.speaker: Council Staff

chapters.summary: Council Member Morano discloses associations with DOE, Lou's Helping Hand, Victory for the Voiceless, Prospect Heights Open Street, Columbia University, CUNY, YMCA, and Italian Club of Staten Island. He thanks Chair Lee for her leadership and votes yes on T2026-2177, 2178, and 2302, but no on all other items.
chapters.title: Council Member Morano discloses conflicts, votes no on most items

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: You're a model public servant.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: And with that, I will vote yes on T2026-2177 and 2178 and T2026-2302 and no on all the rest.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Pardon, thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Members Gennaro and Marte are called and vote. Council Member Brooks-Powers discloses associations with Delta Sigma Theta Sorority and YMCA, and that her child is associated with DOE, Edge School of the Arts, and Community Youth Care Services, voting aye on the budget.
chapters.title: Council Members Gennaro, Marte, and Brooks-Powers vote with disclosures

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers

chapters.summary: The committee clerk announces results: preconsidered resolutions on organizations, community development, expense, revenue, and contract budgets adopted 14-1; interest rate resolutions for 6%, 9%, and 16% adopted 13-2; the 2.5% interest rate and executive capital budget adopted 15-0. All items pass.
chapters.title: Vote outcomes: budget adopted with 14-1 and 13-2 margins

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: All items have been adopted by the committee with the following votes.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Preconsidered resolution.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: In relation to organizations receiving funding, the Community Development Program, the Expense Revenue Contract, and MN9 have been adopted by the committee with 14 in the affirmative, one in the negative, and no abstentions.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Interest rates in relation to 6%, 9%, and 16 have been adopted by the committee with 13 in the affirmative, two in the negative, no abstentions.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Preconsidered resolution interest rate in regards to 2.5% and the executive capital budget have been adopted by the committee with 15 in the affirmative, zero in the negative, and no abstentions.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: That's everything.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Everything's been passed.

chapters.summary: Chair Lee confirms all items have passed, closes out the committee vote, thanks everyone, and congratulates the committee on the adopted budget.
chapters.title: Chair Lee closes committee meeting

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: I think we're good, right?

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay, perfect.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: So with that, that closes out our committee vote.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you, everyone.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Congrats.

summary: Finance Committee Chair Linda Lee leads the vote to adopt the $125.8 billion FY2027 budget, highlighting key investments in parks, libraries, mental health, and nonprofit services.
Council members disclose conflicts of interest during roll call, with most voting aye on all items while Council Members Wong and Morano vote no on certain interest rate and budget resolutions.
All budget resolutions pass with 14-1 and 13-2 margins, adopting the expense, contract, capital, and community development budgets along with property tax rate resolutions.
title: Committee on Finance
